questions.prompt: High School Health - Personal Health and Wellness: which concept is the best anchor for a NYS standards-aligned classroom task?
questions.correctText: wellness

prevention
questions.explanation: wellness is the anchor concept for Personal Health and Wellness. In NYS Health Education, students need to use that concept to reason through a task, not just recognize the word.

ignoring the source or data in the prompt

questions.explanation: The useful move is to connect evidence to nutrition; this matches the standards/CED emphasis on reasoning and application.

questions.prompt: A NYS standards-aligned classroom task asks students to interpret scenario or personal-wellness data for Teamwork and Fair Play. What should they do first?
questions.correctText: Read the prompt, source, or data for the exact task.
choices: Read the prompt, source, or data for the exact task.
memorizing an isolated fact without evidence
making a claim without supporting evidence
choosing an answer because it uses familiar words
questions.explanation: Released NYS tests and AP items often hinge on the exact task wording, source, graph, or scenario.

standardRefs: NYS Physical Education
New York State P-12 Learning Standards
questions.prompt: Which classroom explanation would best connect Teamwork and Fair Play to the larger course skill?
questions.correctText: sportsmanship is useful when students work with others.
choices: ignoring the source or data in the prompt
role should be ignored unless it appears in the title.
sportsmanship is useful when students work with others.
describing a topic without explaining why it matters
questions.explanation: This answer connects a concept to a skill, which is the central move in standards-aligned review.

standardRefs: NYS Physical Education
New York State P-12 Learning Standards
questions.prompt: For Teamwork and Fair Play, which response sounds most like a high-quality constructed response?
questions.correctText: It names sportsmanship, cites evidence, and explains the connection.
choices: making a claim without supporting evidence
It names sportsmanship, cites evidence, and explains the connection.
choosing an answer because it uses familiar words
It lists three vocabulary words without a claim.
questions.explanation: Constructed responses need a claim, evidence, and explanation, whether the item is NYS, AP, or standards-aligned classroom practice.

standardRefs: NYS Physical Education
New York State P-12 Learning Standards
questions.prompt: A student wants to transfer Teamwork and Fair Play knowledge to a new question. What is the best strategy?
questions.correctText: Match the new prompt to sportsmanship, then justify the answer with evidence.
choices: ignoring the source or data in the prompt
Ignore unfamiliar data or wording.
describing a topic without explaining why it matters
Match the new prompt to sportsmanship, then justify the answer with evidence.
questions.explanation: Transfer requires recognizing the underlying concept and then adapting it to the new prompt.

standardRefs: NYS Physical Education
New York State P-12 Learning Standards
questions.prompt: High School Health - Teamwork and Fair Play: which pair best shows how two ideas in the unit connect?
questions.correctText: sportsmanship connects to role.
choices: making a claim without supporting evidence
choosing an answer because it uses familiar words
sportsmanship connects to role.
cooperation replaces all evidence.
questions.explanation: The pair sportsmanship and role belongs together in Teamwork and Fair Play; students should explain the relationship rather than memorize each term alone.

standardRefs: NYS Physical Education
New York State P-12 Learning Standards
questions.prompt: Which answer best describes the role of scenario or personal-wellness data in a High School Health question on Teamwork and Fair Play?
questions.correctText: It provides evidence students must use before deciding.
choices: It only matters if it repeats the vocabulary word.
It provides evidence students must use before deciding.
using a rule from a different unit
memorizing an isolated fact without evidence
questions.explanation: The scenario or personal-wellness data is part of the task. Students should read it for evidence, patterns, constraints, or context before selecting an answer.

standardRefs: NYS Physical Education
New York State P-12 Learning Standards
questions.prompt: Which statement would be easiest to defend in Teamwork and Fair Play?
questions.correctText: sportsmanship can be explained with evidence from scenario or personal-wellness data.
choices: sportsmanship can be explained with evidence from scenario or personal-wellness data.
ignoring the source or data in the prompt
role does not need evidence.
describing a topic without explaining why it matters
questions.explanation: Defensible answers connect a course concept to evidence from the task. That is the common pattern behind NYS, AP, and classroom review questions.

standardRefs: NYS Physical Education
New York State P-12 Learning Standards
questions.prompt: A student misses a NYS standards-aligned classroom task on Teamwork and Fair Play. Which feedback would help most?
questions.correctText: Reread the task, identify sportsmanship, and explain the evidence step by step.
choices: making a claim without supporting evidence
choosing an answer because it uses familiar words
Memorize more unrelated vocabulary before trying again.
Reread the task, identify sportsmanship, and explain the evidence step by step.
questions.explanation: Useful feedback turns the missed item into a process: read the task, identify the concept, and explain evidence.

standardRefs: NYS Physical Education

choose a safe, evidence-based action and explain the health impact.
memorizing an isolated fact without evidence
questions.explanation: Mastery in High School Health means applying the unit idea with a clear reasoning move, especially when the question uses a new source or scenario.
